description.content: Protocol version of the server.
Returns the protocol version supported by the RDF4J Server.
Manage and inspect RDF repositories.
Returns a list of all repositories available on the server.

response.name: SPARQL results with one row per repository.

description.content: Execute a SPARQL query (SELECT, ASK, CONSTRUCT, DESCRIBE) against the repository. The query may be passed via the `query` query parameter.
Execute a SPARQL query or SPARQL Update via POST. Use form parameters `query` or `update`, or send the SPARQL query/update as the request body with the appropriate `Content-Type`.
Create or replace the repository configuration for the specified ID. Body contains an RDF representation of the repository configuration.
Delete the repository and all of its data.
Read and modify the RDF statements in a repository.
Retrieve RDF statements matching an optional triple pattern (subj, pred, obj, context).
Add RDF data to the repository, or apply a SPARQL Update via the form parameter `update`.
Clear the repository (or specified context) and replace its content with the supplied RDF data.
Remove statements matching the optional triple pattern.
Manage namespace prefix declarations.
List all namespace prefix declarations in the repository.

response.name: SPARQL results with prefix and namespace.

description.content: Remove all namespace prefix declarations from the repository.
Get the namespace IRI for a given prefix.
Set the namespace IRI for the given prefix.
Remove the namespace declaration for the given prefix.
Inspect named graphs (contexts) in a repository.
List all named graph (context) IRIs in the repository.
Get the number of statements in a repository.
Returns the number of (explicit) statements in the repository or context.
Group multiple operations in an atomic transaction.
Start a new transaction. The `Location` header of the response holds the transaction URL.

header.description: URL of the new transaction resource.

description.content: Perform an action within the transaction. The action is selected via the `action` query parameter (e.g. `ADD`, `DELETE`, `UPDATE`, `GET`, `SIZE`, `CLEAR`, `COMMIT`, `PING`).
Abort the transaction and discard all changes.

REST API for the Eclipse RDF4J Server. Provides endpoints to list and manage RDF repositories, configure them, query and update their contents using SPARQL, manage namespaces, transactions, contexts and statements, and import/export RDF data in standard serialization formats. The Eclipse RDF4J Server is a Java server application that exposes this protocol over HTTP.
